seoTitle: Create Photo Booth Templates in Canva (Guide)
metaDescription: Learn how to create custom photo booth templates in Canva for weddings, parties, and corporate events, ready to reuse for every client.
intro: Photo booth businesses rely on custom-branded templates for every event, and Canva's drag-and-drop layout tools make it easy to build and reuse these designs quickly. This guide covers creating a template you can adapt for different clients and occasions.

steps.heading: Set the correct print or digital size
steps.body: Create a new design sized to match your photo booth's print output, such as a 2x6 or 4x6 strip.

steps.heading: Design the frame and branding area
steps.body: Add borders, logos, and decorative elements around the space where the event photo will go.

steps.heading: Leave a clear photo placeholder
steps.body: Reserve an empty area or placeholder frame where the actual booth photo will be inserted for each event.

steps.heading: Customize for the event theme
steps.body: Adjust colors, fonts, and graphics to match the specific wedding, party, or corporate event.

steps.heading: Save as a reusable template
steps.body: Keep a master copy of the base design so you can duplicate and tweak it quickly for future clients.

faq.question: Can I reuse the same Canva template for multiple events?
faq.answer: Yes, duplicate the base design and just update the branding and photo placeholder for each new client.

faq.question: How do I insert the actual booth photo into the template?
faq.answer: Drag the event photo onto the placeholder frame in your Canva template, resizing as needed.

faq.question: Can I batch-create templates for several events at once?
faq.answer: Yes, Canva Pro's Bulk Create feature can generate multiple template versions from a data list.

faq.question: Is Canva suitable for print-quality photo strips?
faq.answer: Yes, as long as you export using the PDF Print option and use high-resolution images.
